title: Interview: we meet with Port of Long Beach CEO Dr. Noel Hacegaba
summary: On today’s authoritative episode of Quick Charge, we’re talking all about how ports and port authorities are cleaning up their acts with the man in charge of the busiest port in North America – Port of Long Beach CEO Dr. Noel Hacegaba.

title: BYD is unleashing its full brand power overseas, from luxury EVs to a plug-in pickup
summary: BYD’s sales are surging in overseas markets, including Europe and the UK, but with several new vehicles coming soon, this could be just the start.

title: Volkswagen’s new electric hot hatch breaks cover, but it’s not the ID. Polo GTI
summary: The ID. Polo GTI will go on sale this fall as Volkswagen’s first electric GTI, but another EV hot hatch was just caught testing, and we expect to see it in full by the end of the year.

title: Lucid Gravity GT 2026 Review: drives like a dream with best efficiency and massive range
summary:
I drove the Lucid Gravity GT for more than 547 miles (881 km) across a mix of highway, backroads, and city driving, and I enjoyed nearly everything this vehicle has to offer. For anyone shopping for a luxury electric family vehicle that refuses to compromise on dynamics, the Gravity is clearly a contender, but it is plagued with some software issues that they will hopefully fix soon.

Here is the full review:

title: Hyundai has a few tricks up its sleeve for its next ‘N’ EVs
summary: While everyone is copying Hyundai’s virtual gear shift, introduced in the IONIQ 5 N, the company is already working on the next big thing.

title: Ferrari replaces marketing chief after Luce EV launch backlash
summary:
Ferrari has replaced its long-serving chief marketing and commercial officer, Enrico Galliera, just weeks after the rocky debut of the Luce, the automaker’s first electric car.

The company named former BMW Italy boss Massimiliano Di Silvestre as Galliera’s successor, effective July 1, after a launch that wiped roughly 8% off Ferrari’s stock in a single day.

title: Prime Day-1 Deals: Expanded EcoFlow + Anker SOLIX Prime Day Sales with new lows + tons of extra savings, Lectric e-bikes, more
summary:
Amazon’s Prime Day Sale event is in full swing through the rest of the week, and we’ve got plenty to love packed into today’s Green Deals, while the rest of the major savings can be found in our Green Deals roundup hub here. Our three headliners for this issue are all sitting at the top of our favorite brands list, starting with EcoFlow’s expanded Prime Day Sale lineup, among which you can find offers like the 3,600Wh DELTA Pro Portable Power Station with a FREE 170W 25K power bank down at $1,519. There’s also Anker’s SOLIX Prime Day Sale that is giving us tons of new low prices, like the F3800 Plus Portable Power Station at a new $1,985.50 low. There’s also Lectric’s Better-than-Prime Sale that is giving folks the chance to score the biggest bundles yet on the new XPress2 Long-Range Cruiser + Commuter e-bikes at $1,399 – plus, so much more waiting for you below!

Head below for other New Green Deals we’ve found today and, of course, Electrek’s best EV buying and leasing deals. Also, check out the new Electrek Tesla Shop for the best deals on Tesla accessories.

title: Monarc Tracer redefines commuter e-bikes with dual batteries, premium tech, and a 5-year warranty
summary:
A new e-bike company has entered the increasingly crowded commuter e-bike market, but it’s doing so with a strategy that feels notably different from the usual playbook. The new Monarc Tracer combines a powerful Class 3 commuter platform with premium components, a surprising amount of integrated technology, and one feature that almost no other e-bike company includes as standard: a second battery.

Plus, it comes with the backing of the largest e-bike company in North America.

title: DHL is putting cargo on wind-powered ships across the Atlantic
summary: DHL is turning to VELA for wind power to help move cargo across the Atlantic.

title: The Genesis GV90 finally has a launch date: Here’s when the ultra-luxe SUV is arriving
summary: The full-size luxury SUV is almost here. Genesis confirmed the GV90 will launch by the end of 2026 as expected. Here’s what we know so far.

title: Rivian Discontinues Its Cheapest R1T And R1S As R2 Rolls Out
summary: Rivian flagged earlier this year that the Dual Standard variants of its large SUV and truck were on the way out.

title: This Ex-Rental Tesla's Battery Was Heavily Degraded. Then The Decline Slowed
summary: After losing 20% of its capacity in its first two years, this abused Tesla’s battery degradation appears to have almost plateaued.

title: Rivian Has A Bold Plan For You To Skip The Repair Shop
summary: Rivian says 80% of EV repairs could eventually be handled by its mobile fleet, reducing the need for visits to physical service centers.

title: A Driverless Xiaomi Electric SUV Just Lapped The Ring. We Have Questions
summary: A 10-minute Nurburgring lap time isn't impressive. The fact that there is nobody in the driver’s seat is what makes this unique.

title: Tesla FSD Finally Reached Europe. Now Speed Limits May Slow Its Expansion
summary: Sweden has a problem with FSD breaking the speed limit on demand.

title: A Tesla Model Y Battery Test Looked Bad. Then The Degradation Almost Stopped
summary: After a sharp early drop, this Tesla Model Y's recent battery health test suggests this mostly fast-charged LFP pack has settled down.

title: License Plate Cameras Are About To Start Tracking A Lot More Than Just Your Car
summary: These next-gen ALPR systems look to bridge the gap between you and your car—which means tracking your airpods, wearables, and more.

title: Skipping The Ferrari Luce Could Cost Collectors The Cars They Really Want
summary: Ferrari’s first EV may be controversial, but for loyal collectors, saying no could make the next dream allocation more difficult.

title: The 2027 Porsche Taycan Gets Fake Gear Shifts And A Bigger Standard Battery
summary: The refreshed Taycan has a bigger standard battery and more theater through simulated gears.

title: Uber, Stellantis, And Wayve Want To Blanket The World In Robotaxis
summary: Uber seems to have spent the past year attaching itself to every major robotaxi effort it can, and now Stellantis is joining that list.
